text: The surgical team approaches the thymectomy from the left side due to the patient's anatomical variation, specifically the presence of a left-sided superior vena cava.
The tumor is prominently visible above the surgical instruments, indicating its location relative to the operative field.
The superior vena cava (SVC) is visible with the phrenic nerve lying over it, highlighting the anatomical relationship in the surgical field.
The surgeon uses a Maryland dissector and fenestrated forceps with wristed articulation to efficiently dissect tissues, demonstrating the ease of maneuverability in the surgical field.
The surgeon aligns the instruments to enter the correct anatomical plane for dissection, preparing to continue the thymectomy procedure.
The surgeon, positioned at the patient's side, demonstrates the flexibility of quickly switching between wristed instruments and standard VATS instruments, including the visible energy device.
